title: Add Google, Apple, Microsoft, and Passkey login options on Register Step 2 and Login page

requirements.text: On the Register page Step 2 (Authenticate screen), replace the single 'Verify with Internet Identity' button with four distinct authentication option buttons matching the Internet Identity (id.ai) UI: a Google button (with Google 'G' logo), an Apple button (with Apple logo), a Microsoft button (with Microsoft colored squares logo), and a 'Continue with Passkey' button (with passkey/person icon). Each button, when clicked, must trigger the Internet Identity login flow — the underlying authentication mechanism remains Internet Identity, but the UI presents these four options directly on the screen so users can visually select their preferred method before the popup opens.

quotes: ye photo me dikh raha h na loging karne ka option aisa option tum bhi add karo taki user name mobile no. or bgmi id dalne ke baad ye option aye fir ragisterd done ho jaye

acceptanceCriteria: Step 2 (Authenticate) screen shows four separate buttons: Google, Apple, Microsoft, and Continue with Passkey
Each button has its respective brand logo/icon (Google multicolor G, Apple black logo, Microsoft colored squares, passkey person icon)
The single 'Verify with Internet Identity' button is no longer displayed
Clicking any of the four buttons triggers the Internet Identity authentication flow
After successful authentication, registration proceeds to Step 3 (Success screen) as before
Error state ('Authentication cancelled or failed') still shows if login is cancelled, with option to retry
Layout matches the id.ai 'Choose method' style: Google, Apple, Microsoft in a row, then 'Continue with Passkey' as a full-width button below

requirements.text: On the Login page, replace the existing single Internet Identity authentication button with the same four authentication option buttons: Google (with Google 'G' logo), Apple (with Apple logo), Microsoft (with Microsoft colored squares logo), and 'Continue with Passkey' (with passkey/person icon). Each button triggers the Internet Identity login flow. The underlying authentication mechanism remains Internet Identity.

quotes: Login page — same 4 options directly dikhenge

acceptanceCriteria: Login page shows four separate buttons: Google, Apple, Microsoft, and Continue with Passkey
Each button has its respective brand logo/icon
The single Internet Identity button is replaced by these four options
Clicking any of the four buttons triggers the Internet Identity authentication flow
After successful authentication, user is logged in and redirected to dashboard as before
Layout matches the id.ai 'Choose method' style: Google, Apple, Microsoft in a row, then 'Continue with Passkey' as full-width button below

constraints: Do not change the backend authentication mechanism — Internet Identity must remain the underlying auth provider
Do not modify files in frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts or frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts
The four buttons are UI wrappers that call the same Internet Identity login function already in use
nonGoals: Replacing Internet Identity with direct Google/Apple/Microsoft OAuth integrations
Adding any new backend authentication logic
Changing the registration form (Step 1) or success screen (Step 3)
